ideation: This sequence simulates a singer holding a microphone. The right arm will hold the mic at the mouth level, while the left arm will move in a fluid motion to mimic the way singers gesture. I will use the examples to ensure that the arm movements are natural and expressive. In the first frame, both arms are in a neutral position. The right arm will begin to raise to hold the microphone, while the left arm will remain in a relaxed position. The second frame will show the right arm holding the microphone at mouth level, while the left arm starts to move outward in a dynamic gesture. In the third frame, the left arm will move back to its neutral position. The right arm remains holding the microphone, maintaining the singer's pose. The fourth frame will show the left arm moving outward again, creating a fluid motion and completing the cycle. This sequence will emphasize the dynamic nature of performing, and the slight movements of the left arm will add expressiveness to the pose.

frames.beat: both arms hang relaxed, ready to perform
right arm lifts to cradle the microphone
left arm swings out, a dynamic stage gesture
left arm returns to side, mic held steady
both arms hold the pose, the song continues
